# Bookmark Clipboard — API guide for LLMs

> A self-hosted dashboard of bookmarks (organized in categories) plus a shared
> clipboard of text notes and files. Everything is controllable over a small HTTP
> API. This file tells an AI/agent how to use that API directly — no SDK or MCP
> server required. (An MCP server also ships in the repo under `mcp/` if you
> prefer named tools; a human-friendly reference is at `/docs.html`.)

## Conventions

- Base URL: the origin serving this file (e.g. `http://HOST:PORT`). All calls go to `BASE/api?action=NAME`.
- Requests/responses are JSON, except `upload` (multipart) and `download` (binary).
- Errors: non-2xx responses return `{ "error": "message" }`.
- Auth: none — intended for a trusted LAN.
- Every write bumps a `version` counter; poll `version` to detect external changes.

## Recommended workflow

1. Read current state first: `GET /api?action=bookmarks` and/or `GET /api?action=clips`.
2. Mutate by `id` (bookmarks are `bm_…`, clips are `clip_…`) — more reliable than names.
3. Re-read if you need fresh ids/indices after multi-step changes.

## Data model

- `bookmarks` is an object: category name -> array of bookmarks. Category order = key order; bookmark order = array order.
- A bookmark is EITHER a link tile `{ id, name, url, ping? }` OR an info (click-to-copy) tile `{ id, name, info }` — never both. `ping:true` shows an online/offline dot (link tiles only).
- A clip is `{ id, type:"text"|"file", ..., tags?:[...], todayOnly?, createdAt }`. Text clips have `text` (Markdown); file clips have `filename`, `originalName`, `size`. `todayOnly:true` auto-deletes after today.
- Tags: any clip may carry a `tags` array (0..unlimited strings), de-duplicated case-insensitively.

## Locating a bookmark (for update/delete/move)

Provide ONE of: `id` (best); or `category` + `index` (0-based); or `category` + `name` (first match). No match -> 404.

## Actions

### Read
- `GET /api?action=bookmarks` -> `{ bookmarks, version }`
- `GET /api?action=clips` -> `{ clips, version }`
- `GET /api?action=tags` -> `{ tags:[{name,count}], version }`
- `GET /api?action=config` -> full `{ bookmarks, clips, version }`
- `GET /api?action=version` -> `{ version }`
- `GET /api?action=health` -> runtime/storage diagnostics
- `GET /api?action=help` -> pointers to this file + docs + the action list
- `GET /api?action=download&filename=STORED&original=NAME` -> file bytes

### Bookmarks (JSON body via POST)
- `add-bookmark` `{ category, name, url? , info?, ping?, index? }` — provide url OR info. Creates the category if missing.
- `update-bookmark` `{ <locator>, fields:{ name?, url?, info?, ping? } }` — setting info converts to an info tile; setting url converts to a link tile.
- `delete-bookmark` `{ <locator> }` (POST or DELETE)
- `move-bookmark` `{ <locator>, toCategory?, toIndex? }`
- `reorder-bookmarks` `{ category, order:[ids-or-names] }` (omitted keep original order at the end)
- `bookmarks` `{ "Cat":[ {name,url}, ... ] }` — bulk replace the whole tree (ids preserved/assigned)

### Categories
- `add-category` `{ category, index? }`
- `rename-category` `{ category, newName }`
- `delete-category` `{ category }` (POST or DELETE) — also deletes its bookmarks
- `reorder-categories` `{ order:[names] }`

### Clipboard
- `clip` (alias `add-clip`) `{ text, tags?, todayOnly? }` -> created text clip
- `add-file` `{ filename, contentText? | contentBase64?, tags?, todayOnly? }` -> file clip from JSON (no multipart)
- `upload` (multipart/form-data) fields: `file`, `todayOnly` (1/0) -> file clip
- `update-clip` `{ id, text?, tags?, todayOnly? }` (id may also be `?id=` in the query) -> `{ ok, clip }`; pass `tags:[]` to clear tags
- `delete-clip` (alias `clip`) `{ id }` (POST or DELETE) — removes the stored file too

## Examples

```
BASE="http://HOST:PORT"

# see everything
curl "$BASE/api?action=bookmarks"
curl "$BASE/api?action=clips"

# add a bookmark (link tile) and an info tile (click-to-copy value)
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=add-bookmark" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"category":"AI Tools","name":"Claude","url":"https://claude.ai","ping":true}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=add-bookmark" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"category":"Servers","name":"NAS IP","info":"192.168.1.20"}'

# edit / move / delete a bookmark by id (get ids from action=bookmarks)
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=update-bookmark" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"bm_abc","fields":{"name":"Claude (Opus)","ping":false}}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=move-bookmark" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"bm_abc","toCategory":"Favorites","toIndex":0}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=delete-bookmark" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"id":"bm_abc"}'

# clipboard: add text (Markdown ok) with tags, add a file from JSON, edit, delete
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=clip" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"text":"# Note\n\n- item","tags":["work"]}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=add-file" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"filename":"summary.txt","contentText":"Generated by an agent."}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=update-clip&id=clip_abc" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"tags":["work","urgent"]}'
curl -X POST "$BASE/api?action=delete-clip" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"id":"clip_abc"}'
```
